content: graphology-communities-louvain runs Louvain (modularity max) for directed & undirected graphs, O(|E|), scalable to 100M+ nodes algorithmically. JS benchmarks: 1000 nodes 52ms; 50k nodes / 994k edges ~938ms detecting 43 communities. Single-threaded JS at 1M-5M nodes / tens of millions of edges will be multi-second to tens-of-seconds and may block the main thread — feasible but should run in a Web Worker and be treated as an async one-shot, not interactive. Degree distribution and connected components are O(N+E) and cheap; betweenness centrality is O(NE) and infeasible client-side at this scale (needs sampling/approximation).
